items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative with a $1 million fund to support social groups hosting free or affordable events for young people. The program will operate in New York, Los Angeles and London and is framed as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups.
Fund targets New York, Los Angeles and London.
Money will support free or affordable social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour initiative.
The initiative is presented as addressing Gen Z loneliness.
takeaways: Focused investment in NY, LA and London — these urban markets are the program’s geographic targets.
Corporate funding is being directed toward organizing in-person social events for young people.
The initiative represents a tech company investing in offline, venue-based activities in major cities.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your company's data. The end-to-end demo requires no graph theory or query expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs address AI agents' lack of company-specific knowledge.
Automated pipeline builds a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Connects the graph to an agent that understands your business.
No graph expertise required—aimed at practical, end-to-end implementation.
Includes link to a related episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: Knowledge graphs can make AI agents reliable sources of enterprise-specific information.
Automation reduces the need to hire or train staff in graph theory/query languages.
An end-to-end demo helps shorten time-to-deployment for internal AI use cases.
Using this approach can lower the risk of AI producing inaccurate answers about your organization.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, explains that energy incentives are often top of mind but only part of the tax-credit picture for sustainability. She highlights less obvious options—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovations like reimagining packaging or processes—and argues that viewing sustainability investments more broadly can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax opportunity for sustainability projects.
State and local abatements can provide additional tax support for sustainability investments.
Location-based incentives may influence project siting and financial outcomes.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts (e.g., reimagining packaging or processes).
A broader view of tax incentives can unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability initiatives.
takeaways: Include state and local abatements in site-selection and capital-planning analyses for grid and data center projects.
Evaluate location-based incentives as part of decisions around grid connections, siting, and operational footprint.
Consider R&D tax credits when pursuing process or operational innovations that improve sustainability or efficiency.
Don’t rely solely on energy incentives—screen projects for a wider set of tax credits and incentives to maximize value.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… GeForce Garage presents the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build — a small-form-factor PC by u/JeffersonPutnam featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition in a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis. The description lists full system specs including an MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build for the Summer of RTX community contest.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small-form-factor) with GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition GPU.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB Teamgroup DDR5-6000 RAM.
Power & cooling: Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO used.
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker (link provided in description).
takeaways: High-end desktop GPUs like the RTX 5090 can be accommodated in SFF builds but require a high-capacity SFX PSU (here, Corsair SF1000) — verify power delivery and connector compatibility.
Compact systems need robust CPU cooling (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO used) — assess thermal solutions for sustained workloads in constrained enclosures.
Confirm chassis, motherboard, and GPU physical compatibility (clearance, bracket/slot layout) when deploying large GPUs into small-form-factor cases.
Adopt platform-level compatibility checks (DDR5 support, CPU/motherboard pairing) when specifying modern GPU-equipped workstations for edge or specialized deployments.
Use community build parts lists (PCPartPicker) as practical references for validated component combinations and compatibility checks.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked new revenue streams. Sreeni Alakappan says TTEC used GenAI and Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress its digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was a key benefit cited by TTEC.
The partnership helped compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams were unlocked through deployed AI solutions.
takeaways: Leverage proven cloud infrastructure to speed AI prototyping and deployment.
Cloud consulting can compress transformation timelines and expand project ambitions.
GenAI initiatives can improve developer productivity and streamline operations.
Rapid AI deployment can create new revenue opportunities for service-oriented businesses.
